title: Fort William H. Seward National Historic Landmark, Haines
description: A row of three-story white historic officers' quarters buildings with colorful painted trim at Fort William H. Seward in Haines, Alaska. Completed in 1904 as the last of twelve Gold Rush-era U.S. Army posts in Alaska, decommissioned after WWII, now a National Historic Landmark.
alt: A row of three-story white historic homes with colorful painted trims at Fort William H. Seward, Haines, Alaska
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-11 — downloaded from nps.gov Fort William H. Seward place page, visually confirmed
